name: docs-audit
description: Use to AUDIT existing documentation — score a docs/help/agent-readable surface for friction, drift, accessibility, retrieval, or audience conflict, or debug a docs failure (support tickets, confused onboarding, bad agent tool calls, stale examples, zero-result search). Covers README/quickstart/reference/examples, in-product help/onboarding/microcopy, llms.txt/agent context files/RAG structure, and API/tool-contract descriptions. Triggers on "docs review", "audit our docs/README/help", "why can't users find this", "why does the agent call the wrong tool", "our quickstart fails". Do NOT use to DESIGN or measure new docs from scratch (use docs-design), to judge a code package's developer onboarding — install-to-first-success, errors, packaging (use dx-audit), to only tighten line-level prose of a single piece (use writing-audit), for end-user product UX/accessibility outside help (use ux-audit), or for repo-level agent hardening / AGENTS.md / context-file design (use agent-experience).

inspired_by.name: Research Report — Effective Documentation Patterns and Practices for DX, AX, and UX
inspired_by.author: Informed Skills research synthesis
inspired_by.contribution: Primary synthesis for the skill's cross-audience frame: shared foundations, DX/UX/AX divergences, unique AX requirements, and conflict-resolution patterns.

inspired_by.name: The Documentation System
inspired_by.author: Daniele Procida
inspired_by.contribution: Four-mode documentation taxonomy and the anti-mixing rule for tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation.

inspired_by.name: Docs as Code
inspired_by.author: Anne Gentle and Write the Docs contributors
inspired_by.contribution: Version control, PR review, automated tests, link checks, and CI as the operational baseline for durable documentation.

inspired_by.name: Information Architecture: For the Web and Beyond
inspired_by.author: Rosenfeld, Morville, and Arango
inspired_by.contribution: Organization, labeling, navigation, search, card sorting, and IA vocabulary for docs systems.

inspired_by.name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2
inspired_by.author: World Wide Web Consortium
inspired_by.contribution: Accessibility floor for help and docs: focus visibility, target size, semantic structure, captions, transcripts, and consistent help.

inspired_by.name: Improving API Usability
inspired_by.author: Brad Myers and Jeffrey Stylos
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical grounding for treating examples as primary developer documentation rather than decorative supplements.

inspired_by.name: How to Design a Good API and Why It Matters
inspired_by.author: Joshua Bloch
inspired_by.contribution: API design as documentation: self-documenting interfaces, least astonishment, and easy-to-use/hard-to-misuse contracts.

inspired_by.name: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.0
inspired_by.author: OpenAPI Initiative
inspired_by.contribution: Schema-as-docs model for HTTP APIs, including descriptions and examples that tools and agents consume directly.

inspired_by.name: MCP Tool Descriptions Are Smelly!
inspired_by.author: Md Hasan and coauthors
inspired_by.contribution: Evidence that tool-description quality changes agent task success and that more description can regress behavior when it is not targeted.

inspired_by.name: Agent Skills overview
inspired_by.author: Anthropic
inspired_by.contribution: Progressive disclosure model for agent-readable instructions: metadata always loaded, instructions on trigger, resources on demand.

inspired_by.name: Evaluating repository-level context files for coding agents
inspired_by.author: Gloaguen and coauthors
inspired_by.contribution: Empirical caution that maximalist always-loaded context can reduce task success and increase cost; grounds minimal, measured AX docs.

inspired_by.name: Help and Documentation
inspired_by.author: Nielsen Norman Group
inspired_by.contribution: Help in the moment of need, contextual help, and support for both search-first and browse-first help behavior.

inspired_by.name: Stripe API Errors reference
inspired_by.author: Stripe
inspired_by.contribution: Structured error envelope pattern: machine-readable code, user-facing message, offending parameter, and doc link.

inspired_by.name: Cloudflare documentation for AI tooling
inspired_by.author: Cloudflare
inspired_by.contribution: Markdown-for-agents, retrieval-friendly content, token budgeting, and content negotiation as a way to serve humans and agents from one source.

metadata.catalog_summary: Audit a docs/help/agent-readable surface for friction, drift, accessibility, retrieval, or audience conflict and score it — or debug a concrete docs failure.
